title: CJIS Audit Log Compliance

spl:
index=cjis sourcetype="cjis:query"
| where isnull(officer_id) OR isnull(ncic_transaction_id) OR len(device_id)<4
| stats count by agency, src, query_type
| sort -count
description: Ensures CJIS systems capture user ID, timestamp, device, and action for NCIC/III access as required.
value: Ensures CJIS systems capture user ID, timestamp, device, and action for NCIC/III access as required.
implementation: Monthly completeness report; tamper-evident forwarding; background check status on accounts.
visualization: Table (incomplete records), Line chart (query volume), Compliance gauge (field completeness %).
knownFalsePositives: CJIS query audits that spike during system upgrades, new CAD integrations, and training queries; use CJIS training rosters in triage.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: RMS/CAD, CJIS WAN.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="cjis:query"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Monthly completeness report; tamper-evident forwarding; background check status on accounts.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cjis sourcetype="cjis:query"
| where isnull(officer_id) OR isnull(ncic_transaction_id) OR len(device_id)<4
| stats count by agency, src, query_type
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**CJIS Audit Log Compliance** — Ensures CJIS systems capture user ID, timestamp, device, and action for NCIC/III access as required.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="cjis:query"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): RMS/CAD, CJIS WAN. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cjis; **sourcetype**: cjis:query. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cjis, sourcetype="cjis:query". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(officer_id) OR isnull(ncic_transaction_id) OR len(device_id)<4` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by agency, src, query_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (incomplete records), Line chart (query volume), Compliance gauge (field completeness %).
grandmaExplanation: In our line of business, we pay close attention to "CJIS Audit Log Compliance" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
